text.title: strict-mode-required-fields Failure Modes
text.summary: Five antipatterns that get a schema rejected by the strict-mode compiler at request time.

antipattern.symptom: Field declared `apartment: str = ""` to mean "absent". Downstream code cannot distinguish "no apartment" from "deliberately empty apartment".
antipattern.root-cause: Author avoided `Optional` because they thought strict mode rejects null. It rejects optional, not nullable.
antipattern.fix: Use `apartment: str | None = None`. Strict mode accepts `{"type": ["string", "null"]}` with the field still in `required`.

antipattern.symptom: Strict-mode invoice extraction declares `total: int | None`. Model emits `null` whenever it cannot compute the value, hiding the failure.
antipattern.root-cause: Author over-applied the nullable pattern to fields that should never be absent in valid input.
antipattern.fix: Keep the field non-nullable and pair it with refusal-field-strict-schema (a separate `refusal: str | None` field that the model writes when it genuinely cannot answer).

antipattern.symptom: OpenAI returns `400 Invalid schema for response_format: additionalProperties must be false when strict is true`.
antipattern.root-cause: Pydantic model lacks `ConfigDict(extra="forbid")`. Default Pydantic config does not emit `additionalProperties: false`.
antipattern.fix: Add `model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid")` to every model in the strict-mode tree, including nested ones. Re-emit the schema and re-submit.

antipattern.symptom: OpenAI returns `400 Invalid schema: required list does not match properties`. Some property is declared but not in `required`.
antipattern.root-cause: Pydantic field declared without `= None` default; the bridge marked it non-required while still listing it in `properties`.
antipattern.fix: Either make the field truly required (no default) or convert to `T | None = None` (nullable, default None — bridge keeps it in required).

antipattern.symptom: Strict-mode schema includes `metadata: dict[str, Any]` to accept arbitrary user keys. Bridge emits `additionalProperties: true` somewhere in the tree; strict mode rejects.
antipattern.root-cause: Author conflated "free-form metadata" with "strict-mode schema". The two cannot coexist on the same object.
antipattern.fix: Either drop strict mode (use loose JSON mode) or split: typed sub-object with closed keys + separate `audit_blob: str` field carrying the JSON-encoded freeform.
